rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings For Simone Thomas S Petition For Interpretation Of Trust And Relief From Breach Of Trust Filed February 26, 2016; Supporting Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: Denied. The issues presented in this motion were previously presented by Petitioner's motion for judgment on the pleadings heard and denied on September 6, 2017 (and many of those issues have also been presented (and ruled against) in other motions filed by Petitioner in these related petitions). The Court declines to consider this repetitive second motion for judgment on the pleadings, and the time to file a motion to reconsider has long since passed. Even were the Court to consider the motion on the merits, it would be denied for the same reasons Petitioner's earlier motions have been denied. Should Petitioner further delay these proceedings by filing any future non-meritorious motions raising already-addressed issues, the Court will seriously consider imposing sanctions. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, May 29, 2018.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CRISCI 2005 REVOCABLE TRUST DTD 12/28/2005 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions To Confirm Appointment Of Successor Trustee Comerica Bank
rulings.Rulings: Successor Trustee Comerica Bank's Petition for Instructions to Confirm Appointment of Successor Trustee Comerica Bank is granted. The January 18, 2018 document signed by four beneficiaries purporting to exercise their right under section 4.2(a) of the second amendment to remove and replace the trustee depends for its legitimacy on each of the four signatories being an "income beneficiary" within the meaning of that section. If any one signatory to that document is not an income beneficiary, then the document lacks signatures of a majority of the income beneficiaries. The Court concludes that Marie A. Sorci is not an income beneficiary within the meaning of the trust or Probate Code section 16325. None of what the trust gives to Ms. Sorci satisfies the statutory definition of "a person to whom net income of a trust is or may be payable." Unlike the other six beneficiaries, Ms. Sorci has no right to share in the income or ultimate distribution of the principal of the trust. Her sole right as set out in sections 7.5 and 7.6(a) is to receive certain personal effects and to live rent free in the trust's real property until she dies or decides to relinquish residency. While the trust is required to pay certain expenses related to the property, Ms. Sorci is not entitled personally to receive any payments from the trust during her residency. Nor does the trust entitle her to receive any share of the proceeds from the sale of the property after she no longer resides there. Nor does the trust give her any estate in the trust's real property that would entitle her to lease it to others or otherwise convert its value to personal income for her use. Neither of the proposed hypothetical situations advanced by Respondent in support of including Ms. Sorci as an income beneficiary changes this analysis. The hypotheticals are neither reasonably likely to occur nor provided for under the terms of the trust. Moreover, the trust evidently uses the term "income beneficiary" as a limiting subcategory of all beneficiaries. In several places, when the trust means to include all beneficiaries, such as, for example, the time to object to an accounting (section 4.6) or the limitation on trustee liability (section 4.10), the trust uses the unmodified term "beneficiaries." It is a standard principle of interpretation that each word in a document is to be given meaning and that the drafter's use of different words or phrases has significance. The only person who could be among all beneficiaries but not an income beneficiary is Ms. Sorci. Because Ms. Sorci is not an income beneficiary, the remaining three signatories to the January 18, 2018 document did not constitute a majority of the six total income beneficiaries; therefore, the document failed to effect a removal or appointment of a trustee. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, May 29, 2018.
